gaps.tactic: Desktop reading measure (characters-per-line cap)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Cap prose column to ~60-72 cpl (~55 cpl optimum per Dyson); implemented as a max-width cap of ~34-38em / lg:max-w-[76ch]. The old 34em half-width cap was replaced by 76ch.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.1 gives 'Measure (mobile) ~30-40 cpl, don't shrink type to widen' but has NO desktop/wide-viewport reading measure and no max-width cap for prose columns. Long-running lines on wide screens are a core readability failure the standard never addresses. Fully app-agnostic.

gaps.tactic: Elevated line-height for long-form reading vs UI body
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Treatment bumps prose line-height to 1.7 on wide screens (lg:leading-[1.7]); base prose is leading-relaxed = 1.625. Deliberately higher than the UI body default.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.1 sets body line-height 1.45-1.55 (UI body). It does NOT distinguish a higher reading line-height (1.6-1.7) for long-form prose blocks. A generalizable reading standard should carry a separate, taller line-height band for sustained reading.

gaps.tactic: Inter-paragraph air / paragraph rhythm as a deliberate lever
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Give \n\n breaks real air (visible inter-paragraph spacing) as a presentation-only comfort lever; whitespace/visual chunking lowers perceived effort. Framed as a comfort + perceived-effort win, not comprehension.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.3 has a generic spacing scale and Gestalt grouping (~1:2) but no explicit paragraph-spacing rule for prose blocks. The reading-specific 'give paragraphs air' lever is absent.

gaps.tactic: Distinct typeface roles for prose vs data/chrome
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Narrative prose = sans (Outfit) 16px; data-dense surfaces = mono (JetBrains Mono); 4-role system (Outfit UI / Noto Serif long-form / Montserrat wordmark / JetBrains Mono mono). Prose paragraphs never use mono; mixing a system stack into reading surfaces is a violation.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN says nothing about assigning distinct typefaces by role (reading vs data vs chrome). A generalizable reading/visual standard could carry the principle 'long-form reading text and data/numeric text get distinct, role-appropriate faces' without naming specific fonts.

gaps.tactic: Scanning is the default; the fix for a wall is more pickup points, not fewer words
gaps.boothcheck_detail: 79% scan every new page, 16% read word-by-word (Nielsen 1997). Way out of 'wall of text' is more pickup points + stronger heading hierarchy. Scannable layout +47% measured usability (Morkes & Nielsen).
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN has recall-vs-recognition (scannability as a recognition aid) but no explicit 'users scan, design for scanning' principle for reading surfaces, no scan-vs-read split, and no measured scannable-layout effect size. This is a foundational reading-standard gap.

gaps.tactic: Layer-cake via descriptive subheads; F-pattern is a failure symptom
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Descriptive, visually-standout subheads produce the 'layer-cake' gaze pattern (lands on heading, drops into body) — 'by far the most effective way to scan.' F-pattern appears only when text is unformatted; don't design FOR the F-pattern, break it into layer-cake. Plain summative labels ('Key Takeaways','Valuation','Bear case') beat clever ones. 'Headings are pickup points.'
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN explicitly rejects 'F-pattern-as-law' as folklore but offers NO replacement reading model. The constructive layer-cake / descriptive-heading-as-pickup-point pattern is entirely absent. This is the positive guidance the standard lacks behind its rejection.
gaps.standards_source: VISUAL-DESIGN.md §V tier legend (F-pattern rejected, no replacement)

gaps.tactic: Make section headers stronger pickup points (visual prominence)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Raise header visual prominence (size/weight/contrast/hairline rule) so the scanner's gaze lands; treatment = 16px font-semibold primary color + hairline bottom rule + 6px padding; control = quiet 15px. Label text unchanged. Don't let headers get so loud they compete with prose.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN has multi-channel hierarchy (~3-4 levels) but no reading-specific rule that section headers in long prose must clear a scannability threshold to function as pickup points. Generalizable.

gaps.tactic: Signaling: visual cues that reveal structure and direct attention WITHOUT adding words
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Adding structure + visual emphasis that guides the eye without adding words improves retention (g+=0.53) and transfer (g+=0.33) and LOWERS cognitive load; 103 studies, N=12,201. The one comprehension-adjacent lever that pays off. Applied form of spatial-contiguity / extraneous-load reduction.
gaps.why: Neither standard names signaling/cueing as a lever. FLOW-DESIGN covers decision-flow; VISUAL-DESIGN covers static floors; design.md covers voice. The single highest-value reading mechanism (g+=0.53) has no home. Fully app-agnostic.

gaps.tactic: Signal selectively and correctly; over-signaling backfires; emphasis must track confidence
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Highlight everything = highlight nothing (re-adds load). Mis-cueing actively harms (emphasis is an editorial claim about importance). Emphasis must track epistemic confidence — never uniform, never make the weakest claim the most salient. Fluency inflates perceived truth content-blind, so visual ease must not be uniform.
gaps.why: The governing principle 'visual emphasis must track epistemic confidence, never be uniform' (the fluency-truth guardrail) is absent from all three standards. It is a general visual-honesty rule applicable to any content where claims vary in certainty.

gaps.tactic: BLUF / inverted pyramid: lead with the conclusion, it sustains reading
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Front-loading the conclusion lets scanners get the point AND draws engaged readers into supporting detail; structure-first content is read MORE. The worry that 'giving the answer satisfies them and they leave' is not supported. A 'Key Takeaways' block of 3 dense bullets opens before any prose.
gaps.evidence_tier: STRONG structure / MODERATE the 'more reading' claim — NN/g Inverted Pyramid
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §5 has 'front-load value, attention slopes with scroll depth' and the escalation shape, but no BLUF/answer-first reading principle, and VISUAL-DESIGN §V.3 only says 'front-load highest-value content.' The document-level BLUF pattern (conclusion first, then evidence) is not a named tactic.

gaps.tactic: Answer, don't tease; for strong arguments present facts plainly (suspense reduces processing)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Failure mode: a summary that TEASES ('the surprising reason this is mispriced') is a curiosity gap, not BLUF. For strong arguments, narrative/suspense framing REDUCES argument processing — just present the facts when the facts are your value.
gaps.why: design.md bans hype and 'manufactured drama' (adjacent) but has no positive BLUF rule and no 'answer-not-tease' distinction. The 'present facts plainly for strong arguments' finding is absent.

gaps.tactic: Don't hide the CORE argument behind a blind collapse; A/B default-collapse
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Bull/bear (load-bearing thesis) should render FULL by default; a 5-line peek + blind fade invites satisficing (early exit). If collapsed, replace the blind fade with a verbatim one-line 'what's inside' scent. Collapse only genuinely secondary material. COLLAPSED_LINES=5; collapse trigger lines>5 OR text>400 chars; collapsed maxHeight 7.5em.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN has menu depth ≤2 and 'defer optionals' but no rule about progressive disclosure of READING content specifically — i.e. never collapse the core argument, and replace blind fades with honest scent. The reading-specific application is missing.

gaps.tactic: Section-aware outline / you-are-here (orientation, NOT a progress bar)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Add a section-aware outline derived from real sections; desktop sticky rail, mobile collapsible 'contents'; jump links + active-section highlight on scroll (IntersectionObserver). Renders only with >=3 sections. Answer to 'should we add a % progress bar': NO — ship orientation (you-are-here), not motivation. Rail 13px mono, 168px wide, active = accent + bold + 2px tick.
gaps.why: Neither standard has a wayfinding/table-of-contents pattern for long documents. FLOW-DESIGN debunks the reading-progress bar implicitly (Zeigarnik/gamification) but offers no positive 'orientation outline' replacement. Fully generalizable to any long-form reading surface.
gaps.standards_source: none (FLOW debunks progress-bar gamification, no replacement)

gaps.tactic: Reading-progress / % read bar is rejected as motivational chrome
gaps.boothcheck_detail: No study links a reading progress bar to completion; the only rigorous progress-bar experiments (Conrad 2010, survey completion) show neutral-to-harmful on long slow tasks. Reads as consumer-blog chrome on a serious product. Endowed/fake pre-filled progress is a documented dark pattern. Replaced by honest outline.
gaps.evidence_tier: REJECTED — Conrad et al. 2010; goal-gradient wrong-domain
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN endorses 'endowed progress, seed 1/N' and 'goal-gradient sub-goals 3-5' for COMMITTED ACTIONS — but does NOT carve out that for READING (a non-committed, browsing-like activity per its own Step 0-C) a reading-progress bar is inappropriate and goal-gradient evidence doesn't transfer. This is a real tension the standard should resolve: the seed-1/N progress lever is action-specific, not reading-applicable.

gaps.tactic: Resumption-as-reentry: serve the Ovsiankina resumption tendency with visible re-entry points
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Zeigarnik RECALL is debunked (~null); the Ovsiankina RESUMPTION tendency replicates — people return to an interrupted task. Make it EASY to RESUME a set-down document (re-entry points, you-are-here outline), don't manufacture cliffhangers. Beacon detects resume after >30s idle (RESUME_IDLE_MS=30000).
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §2 Layer III cites the resumption tendency (~67%) and debunks Zeigarnik — but frames it for task flows ('show partially-complete work'). The reading-specific application — easy re-entry into a long document a reader set down — is not stated. Closely covered but reading-application missing.

gaps.tactic: Attention is the currency, not pageviews; attention is front-loaded and decays with depth
gaps.boothcheck_detail: 55% of pageviews got <15s attention; ~3 min attention → ~2x return likelihood (2B visits). ~57% of viewing time above the fold, ~74% in first two screenfuls; attention decays continuously with depth. The '57%' is a viewport artifact — carry the GRADIENT (front-load payoff, reward the scroll), not the number. Readers scroll to ~50% of an article typically.
gaps.evidence_tier: STRONG pattern, soft decimals — Chartbeat/Haile 2014; NN/g 2018
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.3 says 'attention slopes down with scroll depth' (one clause). The richer empirical model — attention not pageviews, front-loaded with a decaying gradient, reward-the-scroll — is absent. Generalizable to any scrollable reading content.

gaps.tactic: Length is fine if structured; engaged time rises with word count
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Long-form (1,000+ words) drew ~2x engaged time of short-form (~123s vs ~57s cellphone, up to ~270s for 5,000+ words); engaged time rose monotonically with word count; long-form pulled similar visitor counts. Length did not suppress attention/reach (survivorship caveat: shows well-structured long-form holds the engaged reader longer, not 'make things longer').
gaps.why: No standard addresses content length at all. The empirical 'length is not the enemy; structure decides' finding is a useful counterweight to a naive 'shorter is better' instinct and is app-agnostic for any reading surface.

gaps.tactic: Multimedia principle: words + RELEVANT graphics beat words alone
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Words plus relevant explanatory graphics integrated with the text lift transfer >1 SD in controlled studies. Conditional on the graphic being explanatory (carrying the argument).
gaps.why: No standard covers integrating graphics with reading text. A general reading/visual standard could carry the multimedia + spatial-contiguity principles. App-agnostic.

gaps.tactic: Inline sparklines / datawords; split-attention: co-locate figure with its sentence
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Word-sized intense graphics ('datawords', Tufte) embed anywhere a number can. Co-locating a figure with the sentence that discusses it removes integration load (split-attention/spatial-contiguity). Keep citations and micro-charts NEXT TO the claim, not in a distant footer. Hold y-baseline constant across comparable glyphs; show last/min/max so magnitude isn't misread.
gaps.evidence_tier: CRAFT/STRONG — Tufte; Chandler & Sweller 1991; Mayer & Moreno 2003
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.5 says 'separate sections with space + subheader' (chrome) but never addresses inline data visualization or spatial contiguity of figure-to-claim. A reading-and-data standard should carry these. App-agnostic.

gaps.tactic: Seductive-details hard negative: decorative images reduce retention
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Interesting-but-irrelevant images REDUCE retention, harm persists even when they don't disrupt flow (diverts processing). The 'one image every N words' rule is unsourced SEO folklore that imports seductive-details damage. A graphic earns its place only by carrying the argument; a decorative image to 'break up text' is a measured drag.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN rejects several visual folklore items but not 'decorative imagery to break up text' / 'one image every N words.' The seductive-details effect is a strong, generalizable negative the standard omits.
gaps.standards_source: VISUAL-DESIGN.md §V tier legend (folklore list — this item absent)

gaps.tactic: Drop caps / ornamental initials rejected (no evidence, gimmick risk)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Drop caps / ornamental initials have no controlled evidence of reading or wayfinding benefit and carry the highest 'lifestyle-magazine gimmick' risk. Use small-caps lead-ins or spacing for section entry instead.
gaps.why: Minor but generalizable typographic-ornament rejection absent from VISUAL-DESIGN's folklore list.

gaps.tactic: Disfluency / 'memory' fonts rejected (Sans Forgetica failed replication)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: The 'desirable difficulty' / disfluency-aids-memory claim failed replication (Xie meta recall d=-0.01); disfluency only makes reading feel harder with no payoff — the opposite of the goal.
gaps.why: A clean, generalizable debunked-reading-myth (harder-to-read fonts do not aid memory) absent from VISUAL-DESIGN's folklore list. Reinforces the 'maximize fluency' direction the standard otherwise implies.

gaps.tactic: Surface verbatim primary-source quotes inline with a restrained marker
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Render verbatim source quotes (⟦⟧ filing excerpts) visibly inline for all readers — a quiet quotation tick / hairline / dotted underline reading as 'verbatim from the source', distinct from bold emphasis, with source metadata on hover. Reader impl: 1px dotted accent underline, cursor:help, 'Verbatim quote' tooltip; NOT the admin yellow highlight (miscues importance). Marking the in-flow quote beats a separate callout (avoids pull-quote duplication).
gaps.why: No standard covers how to present citations/quotes/evidence in reading text. This generalizes to any evidence-bearing reading surface (research, docs, journalism): mark verbatim source material with a restrained, distinct, non-emphasis treatment.

gaps.tactic: Co-locate citations with the claim (Tufte sidenotes); link out to authoritative sources
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Render footnote/source reference as a margin SIDENOTE beside the referencing line on wide screens (Tufte pattern), numbers/text unchanged; degrade to end-of-section footnote list on narrow screens. xl-only gutter at left:calc(100% + 2rem), width 150px, 10px mono. Linking out to authoritative/primary sources is a top trust builder ('more credible than anything you can say yourself'). Place citation where the claim is, not orphaned in a footer.
gaps.why: Spatial-contiguity applied to citations (sidenotes, claim-adjacent sourcing) and the 'link out to sources' trust lever are absent. Generalizable to any sourced reading content.

gaps.tactic: Prominence-Interpretation: a trust signal contributes ZERO until NOTICED
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Every trust asset must clear the noticing threshold — citations visible where the claim sits, methodology link reachable (not a footer orphan), byline visible. A buried trust page scores like the ignored privacy policy (<1% of comments despite users claiming it mattered — the say-do gap). Measure whether trust-dense content is actually noticed (hover-rate, open-rate).
gaps.evidence_tier: MODERATE — Fogg 2003 PIT + STRONG say-do gap
gaps.why: A general principle (a trust/credibility signal must be prominent enough to be noticed, and self-reported importance ≠ behavior) absent from all standards. Applies to any product surfacing credibility cues.

gaps.tactic: Decorative pull-quotes that duplicate prose are rejected (cause light scanning)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: NN/g eye-tracking: readers ABANDON linear reading and fall into light scanning on hitting a pull-quote; verbatim duplication double-reads for screen readers and re-shows a just-read sentence. A margin callout, if ever used, must be pulled FORWARD (a verbatim answer-preview of content not yet reached), never a repeat. boothcheck wired a 'deck' slot but ships it null (a heuristic pull can't guarantee non-tease/non-duplicate).
gaps.why: No standard addresses pull-quotes. The 'duplicate pull-quotes cause light scanning; only pull-forward, never repeat' rule is a clean generalizable reading-layout caution.

gaps.tactic: Curiosity is real but gap-framing backfires; use only immediately-closed micro-gaps
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Curiosity follows an inverted-U, activates reward circuitry, improves memory for incidental nearby material — but curiosity-GAP framing backfires at scale (Upworthy: vagueness helped 8.7%, hurt 50.9% of 8,977 A/B tests; it's about clicks not sustained reading). In a credibility product, overt curiosity-gap framing reads as clickbait. Use only as a genuine question the next sentences actually answer.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §6 debunks Zeigarnik and §7 bans 'false urgency.' But the specific 'curiosity gaps / teaser framing backfire; safe form is an immediately-closed micro-gap' guidance for reading content is not stated. design.md bans 'manufactured drama' (adjacent). Generalizable.

gaps.tactic: Lede + nut-graf two-beat opening
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Open a section: S1 = LEDE (specific concrete hook, NOT the thesis yet, an observation that invites in); S2-3 = NUT GRAF (make the thesis explicit, why it matters, why now); P2+ = body evidence. Distinct from point-first; used for narrative/long-form. Contrast with the BLUF point-first lead (point in sentence 1) used for scannable formats.
gaps.evidence_tier: CONVENTION — WSJ long-form structure; Smart Brevity
gaps.why: design.md covers sentence-level rhythm but has NO document/paragraph opening-structure rule (lede+nut-graf, or point-first). This is a generalizable user-facing-writing structure gap.

gaps.tactic: Arrival sentence not topic sentence; cut throat-clearing
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Open a section with the implication/bet/tension (arrival sentence), never with a throat-clearing topic sentence that announces the obvious ('[Company] generates $X in revenue'). State the sharpest observation in sentence 1; intro 2-4 sentences then into substance. Burying-the-point (point in sentence 3) is the #1 named failure mode.
gaps.evidence_tier: CONVENTION — Boyle×Feynman craft; Economist; Smart Brevity
gaps.why: design.md says 'never open on a wall of numbers' (Feynman) — adjacent — but has no anti-throat-clearing / arrival-sentence / point-first rule and no 'burying the point' failure mode. Generalizable.

gaps.tactic: Frame-then-evidence (argumentative) vs data-then-conclusion (descriptive) — two section modes
gaps.boothcheck_detail: In argumentative sections (bull/bear) state the thesis first, then evidence ('frame, then evidence'). In descriptive sections (valuation, data) state the data and let the reader draw conclusions (no persuasive framing). Two distinct modes for two distinct section jobs.
gaps.why: design.md has one voice for all copy; it does not distinguish argumentative vs descriptive section modes. Generalizable for any writing with mixed section types.

gaps.tactic: Landing-sentence pacing after dense passages
gaps.boothcheck_detail: After a dense/stat-heavy passage insert a short sentence (<10 words) that consolidates the point and lets the reader process before the next beat — a pacing/consolidation function distinct from the reframe. Example: 'Not as alarming as the raw number reads. Not to be dismissed either.'
gaps.why: design.md §3 names short landing sentences (3-10 words) and the reframe/clarity-kick but folds in the function; it does not name the distinct PACING/CONSOLIDATION role of a short line after a dense passage. Closely covered, role under-specified.

gaps.tactic: Average sentence-length target with explicit variance bands
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Aim 14-16 word average (research) / 14-18 (shipped); <20 ceiling. Explicit tiers: short landing 3-10 words; medium analysis 12-18; long complex-clause 22-28. (Flesch-Kincaid grade-10-11 target was considered and REJECTED — model can't compute it reliably; sentence length is the controllable proxy.)
gaps.why: design.md §3 mandates VARYING length and names 3-10-word landings but gives NO average target and no medium/long bands. The measurable readability target (avg 14-18, bands 3-10/12-18/22-28) is the most actionable missing number.

gaps.tactic: Translate-before-you-cite; numbers-with-context; jargon-translate-or-delete
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Translate a concept into plain terms BEFORE citing the number; numbers arrive in supporting clauses, never as the opening clause. Every load-bearing number gets a scale/comparison anchor ('$2B, roughly all the profit our insurance operations generated in the 1990s'). Untranslated jargon ('secular tailwind','TAM expansion') is translated or deleted, never used raw.
gaps.boothcheck_source: NEWSLETTER per-bet shape (L94); RHETORIC §3 (L92); comparison-snippet Feynman (L15)
gaps.evidence_tier: CONVENTION — Feynman one-beat; SEC plain-English; Buffett
gaps.why: design.md §2 has the Feynman 'don't open on numbers' rule — adjacent — but not the explicit translate-THEN-cite ordering, the every-number-gets-a-context-anchor rule, or the jargon-translate-or-delete gate. Generalizable to any number-bearing copy.

gaps.tactic: Specialist-defensibility test; incentive framing; anticipate-and-answer objection; reductio humor; consequence-framing pathos
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Specialist-defensibility: no sentence reads wrong to a domain expert; hedge or omit what you're unsure of. Incentive framing: name who benefits / who pays. Anticipate-and-answer: embed the reader's implicit objection then answer it ('a reader might ask...'). Logical-reductio humor: state an absurd premise, walk it to its flat conclusion, no wink; humor budget ~0-1 per piece. Pathos via consequence-framing: tie a finding to its concrete reader consequence. Ethos/logos/pathos balance — pathos is the most-missing dimension.
gaps.why: design.md says humor comes 'from the fact itself' (adjacent to reductio) but has no specialist-defensibility test, incentive framing, anticipate-objection, humor budget, or pathos/consequence-framing. A richer rhetoric layer the voice standard lacks. Mostly generalizable; some (incentive framing) lean finance but generalize to any 'why did X happen' explanation.

gaps.tactic: Enumerated variation axes across a series; theme-revisit from a new door; voice/promise constant vs theme/structure/angle variable
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Rotate SPECIFIC axes across a series: opening move, thesis placement, evidence cited, structural-humor frequency, paragraph count, closing move, outlook phrasing (7 axes). A recurring theme may repeat as long as each visit enters from a new angle and references how the prior take aged. Voice + promise constant; theme, structure, angle vary. Vary paragraph count by depth, not a fixed count.
gaps.evidence_tier: CONVENTION — boothcheck variation spec; Levine; Housel
gaps.why: design.md §7 has the 3+-recurrence banned-template threshold (COVERED) and 'rotate entry point and sentence shape' but does NOT enumerate the fuller axis set (thesis placement, paragraph count, closing move, humor frequency), the across-time theme-revisit mechanism, or the constant/variable (voice+promise vs theme/structure/angle) split.

gaps.tactic: Extended short-word list + filler kill-list + insincerity test + multiple-negatives ban
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Adds to the Orwell list: approximately→about, additional→more, initiate→start (keep necessary Latinate precision terms: depreciation/amortization/consolidation). Filler kill-list adds: 'very','really','in order to','the fact that' (beyond design.md's 'it's worth noting/one could argue/needless to say'). SEC: cut weak verbs/abstract words; no multiple negatives. Insincerity test: vague/hedged prose signals the writer hasn't figured out their meaning — commit or state the uncertainty.
gaps.evidence_tier: EMPIRICAL — Orwell; SEC Plain English Handbook
gaps.why: design.md §4-5 covers the core Orwell short-word swaps and bans 3 filler hedges; it lacks the extra swaps, the 'very/really/in order to/the fact that' kill-list, the multiple-negatives ban, and the insincerity diagnostic. Generalizable word-level extensions.

gaps.tactic: Extended banned-cliché list
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Adds to the banned-metaphor list beyond design.md: 'flexing muscles','pulling ahead of the pack','breathing down its neck','execution risk','flawless execution'. (design.md already bans headwinds/tailwinds/silver bullet/double-edged sword/taking its toll/rising tide etc. — overlap noted.)
gaps.why: design.md §5 has a banned-cliché list that overlaps but omits the 'flexing muscles / pulling ahead of the pack / breathing down its neck / execution risk' items. Minor additive gap.

gaps.tactic: Runnable prose-gate script as enforcement (not a manual reminder)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Em-dash ban, repeated opening/closing-shape check, and banned-template + smuggling-vocab sweep are enforced by a runnable script: node scripts/prose-gate.mjs <file>; exit 0 = clean; wraps lint-prose-variation.mjs. Variation enforced via a canonical angle deck + deterministic assignAngles(items, DECK, {seed}).
gaps.why: design.md's Provenance MENTIONS that boothcheck enforces the em-dash ban with a gate, but design.md carries no gate/command and no deterministic variation mechanism. A generalizable standard could recommend automated enforcement of mechanical rules. Tooling is boothcheck-specific but the principle (gate mechanical rules) generalizes.

gaps.tactic: Multi-pass ordered edit checklist (accuracy first, polish last) + read-aloud test
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Editing run as distinct ordered passes: A accuracy/no-smuggling → B em-dash+punctuation → C concision/cut → D voice+variation → E read-aloud+scannability. 'Order matters: accuracy first, polish last.' Read-aloud at speaking pace; rewrite anything that trips the tongue (rejected as a model PROMPT rule, kept as a human EDIT step). Bad-draft-fast then iterate; pull real numbers/facts BEFORE writing; cut padding (tight 4 > soft 6).
gaps.evidence_tier: CONVENTION — Economist Style Guide; Paul Graham; SEC
gaps.why: design.md is a flat rule list (rules on the output) with no editing PROCESS, pass ordering, or read-aloud test. A generalizable writing standard could carry an ordered edit-pass discipline.

gaps.tactic: Edit-don't-regenerate: surgical sentence-level substitution with structured edit records
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Fix post-generation failures with surgical sentence-level find/replace, not regeneration; each edit carries section/find/replace/reason/severity/confidence as JSON. Severity ∈ {factual, consistency, tone, clarity, methodology-leak, em-dash}; confidence ∈ {high,medium,low}. Don't restructure sections — edits are substitutions.
gaps.why: No standard addresses the editing-of-generated-text workflow. Generalizable for any AI-assisted writing pipeline (surgical edits over regeneration), though the JSON schema is boothcheck-specific.

gaps.tactic: Every number sourced; anti-smuggling; approximation-is-not-a-fix; implausibility-is-diagnostic; cross-section consistency; period-labeling
gaps.boothcheck_detail: No fabricated figures — every number traces to provided data/cited source/defensible lookup; never cite recalled training-data numbers; hedging a recalled number with 'roughly' does NOT make it sourced. A broken-looking figure (e.g. 2,900x P/E off a near-zero denominator) is OMITTED, not cited or hedged. Same number/claim must agree across all sections. Label the period/unit of every forward/consensus figure; never mix units silently.
gaps.why: design.md §8 ('honesty about limits') is adjacent but carries no anti-smuggling / no-recalled-numbers / approximation-is-not-a-fix / cross-section-consistency / unit-labeling rule. Highly generalizable for any LLM-authored factual reading content; this is a major accuracy gap.

gaps.tactic: No methodology/internal-plumbing self-reference in reader-facing prose
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Reader-facing sections must not cite the product's own internal machinery as if it were a finding (Monte Carlo iteration counts, consensus scores, model weights, seeds, backtest accuracy). Strip the self-reference while preserving the claim. Keep mechanism references in a methodology section only.
gaps.why: design.md says the general spirit 'don't leak internal plumbing into user copy still holds' but explicitly drops the specific list as finance-specific. The general no-internal-plumbing-in-prose principle is present in design.md (so partly covered); the concrete leak taxonomy + strip-while-preserving pattern is not generalized.

gaps.tactic: Instrument reading behavior: engaged (active) time + scroll depth + per-section reached
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Honest metric is ACTIVE time — rolling ~5s window kept alive by scroll/mousemove/keydown/touch/wheel, paused on tab-blur (TICK_MS 1000, ACTIVE_WINDOW_MS 5000); raw time-on-page is not honest. Scroll depth: max depth + 25/50/75/100% milestones AND per-section 'reached' flags via IntersectionObserver — per-section is the metric that matters ('did they reach Catalysts/Sources' = read-through proxy). Bounce = <15s engaged AND <25% depth (Chartbeat drive-by signature) as a guard not a target. Resume = activity after >30s idle. Segment by logged-in/anon AND desktop/mobile. 2-4 weeks baseline.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §8 has a measurement frame for TASK flows (task success, engagement, retention, happiness) but NO reading-specific metrics: engaged-time method, scroll-depth buckets, per-section read-through, bounce signature. This is the entire 'what to measure for reading' apparatus, fully generalizable.

gaps.tactic: Measure reading, not belief; never optimize for 'feels credible'; one flag per change, isolate-then-stack
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Success metrics are behavioral (engaged time, scroll depth, read-through, expansion); deliberately do NOT make 'feels more credible/confident' a KPI — fluency inflates perceived truth content-blind, so a credibility KPI rewards the manufactured-conviction failure. Don't optimize for shares (≈zero correlation with reading) or activity. One feature flag per intervention; randomize at view level; isolate effects then test stacks. A null is informative; pre-register primary metric + minimum detectable effect; only effortful changes gate on a positive result.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §8 has 'measure per-user, the happiness survey guards engagement/satisfaction divergence, use cheap directional A/Bs' (adjacent, COVERED in spirit) but lacks the reading-specific 'measure reading not belief / never KPI feels-credible / shares≠reading / isolate-then-stack' specifics.

gaps.tactic: The unifying bright line: a device may help UNDERSTAND, never manufacture ACTIVITY/ATTENTION/URGENCY
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Codified verbatim: 'A device may help the user UNDERSTAND; it may never manufacture ACTIVITY, ATTENTION, or URGENCY.' The line for gamification is the OBJECT of the reward: understanding/comprehension (in) vs activity/attention (out). Gamifying LEARNING is allowed; gamifying activity is not. Trust-building and dark-pattern-avoidance are one discipline.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §7 has the engagement-vs-usefulness line and the do/don't lists (strongly COVERED) but does not phrase the understand-vs-manufacture-activity bright line, nor the nuance that gamifying COMPREHENSION (vs activity) is acceptable. A sharpening of the existing ethics core.

gaps.tactic: Regulatory bright-line for YMYL/finance: gamification/urgency/fake-social-proof are not just bad but litigated
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Confetti/badges/points/streaks (MA v. Robinhood $7.5M settlement; SEC DEP rulemaking; Langvardt & Tierney 'Confetti Regulation'); fake urgency/countdowns (FTC §5/UCPD/DSA; Mathur 2019 ~11K sites); fake social proof (FTC v. Credit Karma $3M); confirmshaming/forced-continuity/drip-pricing (FTC v. Amazon $2.5B). Raw small real numbers ('join 80 investors') backfire as negative social proof (Cialdini). Decorative trust seals are weak-to-null and sometimes backfire.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §7 bans these on ethical grounds (COVERED in principle) but does not flag the regulatory/legal dimension, the negative-social-proof backfire of small real numbers, or the trust-seal null/backfire. Mostly YMYL/finance-specific but the negative-social-proof and trust-seal findings generalize.

gaps.tactic: Two-sided messaging: disclose a real limitation to RAISE credibility
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Admitting a weakness raises credibility/persuasion (Eisend 2006 meta), strongest when the negative is placed LAST, is lower-importance, reads as VOLUNTARY, and is REFUTATIONAL (raise it then address it). Largest gains when the source is presumed biased. Express uncertainty as a BOUNDED NUMERIC range (trust cost d≈-0.12 to -0.15) not verbal hedging (d≈-0.55).
gaps.why: design.md §8 'honesty about limits / say the limit out loud' is the same spirit (COVERED) but lacks the placement rules (negative last, refutational), the presumed-bias amplifier, and the bounded-numeric-range-beats-verbal-hedge quantification. Generalizable sharpening.

gaps.tactic: First impression in ~50ms; clean error-free presentation is a first-order trust lever; speed gates abandonment
gaps.boothcheck_detail: 'Design look' = most-cited credibility factor (46.1% of comments, Fogg 2003). First impressions form ~50ms and stick (Lindgaard 2006); visual sloppiness reads as analytical sloppiness. Be prototypical in structure, differentiated in substance (low complexity + high prototypicality judged most appealing, detectable at 17ms). Load 1s→3s raises bounce +32%; 1s→10s +123%; a 0.1s speedup +8.4% conversion.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.6 cites 'first impressions favor low complexity + high prototypicality' (COVERED, the prototypicality half) but lacks the 50ms first-impression, the design-as-credibility-halo (clean presentation = perceived rigor), and the load-time-gates-abandonment data. FLOW-DESIGN has the 400ms acknowledgment but not page-load bounce thresholds. Generalizable.

gaps.tactic: Concreteness; reader agency over an assumption; named accountable byline; methodology transparency (layered)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Concrete specific language causes higher satisfaction + spend (+1 SD concreteness ≈ +9% satisfaction, ≥+13% spend; Packard & Berger 2021). Letting the reader modify an imperfect model 'even when severely restricted' sharply raises willingness to use it (Dietvorst 2018) — highest-ROI trust lever. A byline that leads to author background (expertise ~16% of persuasion variance; an INVENTED expert persona trips the deceptive-creators floor). Methodology transparency raises trust (+21% purchase, +22% sales) but has an inverted-U (over-disclosure = TMI) — layer it: plain logic on top, mechanics one click down.
gaps.why: None of the standards carry concreteness-causes-satisfaction, give-the-reader-bounded-agency, accountable-byline, or layered-methodology-transparency. These are general credibility levers for any informational reading product (with the YMYL caveat that an invented persona is forbidden).

gaps.tactic: Percentage-points vs percent; denominator labeling; null-pill dropping; plain-language duration
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Difference between two percentages is percentage points (render '+2.7pp', never '%') — mixing % into a row of levels misstates magnitude (made one stock look ~4x worse). Label every comparator by denominator basis (mid-cycle vs trailing vs now); never show a bare negative trailing margin as a level. When a field is null, omit its pill — never render an em-dash gap inside a claim sentence (reads as broken). Replace cryptic '11.3y must persist' with plain duration ('hold a 9.7% margin for ~11 years').
gaps.why: No standard covers numeric presentation honesty (pp-vs-%, denominator labeling, null handling, plain-language duration). Generalizable to any data-bearing reading surface. Note: design.md uses em-dash as the null placeholder (UI-UX-DESIGN-REFERENCE L29) — boothcheck's 'drop null pills, don't render em-dash gaps in claim sentences' is a related refinement.

gaps.tactic: One color ladder only; reserve color for the rarity/severity scale, keep the gauge neutral; most-colored ≠ largest
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Avoid double-encoding: a from→to gauge is a single neutral accent tint regardless of sign (sign read from marker position + signed value, not color); the only color ladder belongs to a severity/rarity chip (4-rung green→red rarity ramp #34D399/#FBBF24/#FB923C/#FB7185), explicitly a rarity scale not a valence/verdict scale. The most-colored element is NOT the largest (capping saliency avoids implying a buy/sell verdict — a YMYL hazard). Mute the chip when comparison data is sparse (>=2 of 4 distances null).
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.4 has 'never color alone' and 'restrained accent' (adjacent) but no single-color-ladder / no-double-encoding rule, no 'mute the signal when data is sparse,' and no 'cap the most-colored element's size to avoid implying a verdict.' Generalizable visual-encoding-honesty rules.

gaps.tactic: Information Foraging: readers leave a patch when marginal value drops; cues must be accurate (deceiving scent is worse than weak)
gaps.boothcheck_detail: Readers forage and leave when marginal value drops below their alternatives (Marginal Value Theorem). Information SCENT = proximal cues (link labels, headings, preview text) predicting unseen content value; strong scent → press on. Satisficing: readers stop at 'good enough.' A 'deceivingly strong' scent that over-promises is WORSE than weak scent — manufactures pogo-sticking/abandonment; every wayfinding cue must be accurate (a label/jump-link that doesn't deliver). Don't collapse a graded signal into a binary (honesty).
gaps.evidence_tier: STRONG for navigation — Pirolli & Card 1999; Chi 2003
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §4 Step 2 invokes 'optimize information scent, users forage, each label predicts its payoff' for NAVIGATION/menus (COVERED for between-page). It does NOT extend scent to WITHIN-document sustained reading (headings/outline labels must accurately predict section payoff) or state the 'deceiving scent worse than weak' caution. Generalizable extension.

gaps.tactic: Reading treatments are wide-screen only; mobile stays full-width and byte-identical
gaps.boothcheck_detail: All prose treatments (constrained measure, line-height bump, sidenotes, outline rail) apply at lg+/xl+ only; mobile stays full-width and identical to control. Outline degrades to a collapsible 'contents' bar on mobile; sidenotes degrade to an end-of-section footnote list below xl. Mobile root-font boost (@media max-width:640px{html{font-size:18px}}) offsets px floors on narrow viewports.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN handles mobile floors and reflow but has no principle that reading-comfort treatments (measure cap, sidenotes, rails) are wide-screen affordances that must degrade gracefully on mobile. A useful generalizable responsive-reading rule.

gaps.tactic: Recall vs recognition; cap recall not recognition
gaps.boothcheck_detail: boothcheck's 'the way out of a wall is more pickup points, not fewer words' rests on the same recognition-not-recall logic.
gaps.why: FLOW-DESIGN §1 carries recall-vs-recognition explicitly ('Cap recall. Never cap recognition. The fix is more recognition support — scannability — not fewer items'). The boothcheck scannability findings are an application of an already-present principle.

gaps.tactic: Em-dash ban with rewrite alternatives
gaps.boothcheck_detail: boothcheck bans em-dashes in UI strings/prompts (em-dash OK only as null placeholder) and enforces via prose-gate.
gaps.why: design.md §1 carries the full em-dash ban with rewrite alternatives and exceptions. (Only the enforcement MECHANISM and the em-dash-as-null-placeholder convention are gaps — see G38, G50.)

gaps.tactic: Buffett clarity-kick: close a dense passage with a reframing one-liner
gaps.boothcheck_detail: boothcheck names this as a discrete closing technique (reframe not restate; reward after the work).
gaps.why: design.md §3 carries 'close a dense passage with a short line that reframes rather than restates (the Buffett clarity-kick)' verbatim in spirit. Confirmed covered; only the distinct landing-sentence-pacing role (G31) is under-specified.

gaps.tactic: Variation discipline: 3+-recurrence banned template
gaps.boothcheck_detail: boothcheck uses the same 3+-recurrence threshold, additionally machine-checked by the prose-gate.
gaps.why: design.md §7 carries 'if one shape recurs three or more times, it is now a banned template' exactly. The fuller enumerated-axes set and across-time theme-revisit (G35) and the gate enforcement (G38) are the genuine gaps.

gaps.tactic: No-card reading aesthetic: white space > hairline > card; shadows only on floating UI
gaps.boothcheck_detail: boothcheck's audit enforces hairline-rule-plus-spacing over bordered cards, 8px radius cap, shadows on floating UI only, no hover lift/scale — matching the standard.
gaps.why: VISUAL-DESIGN §V.5 carries 'White space > hairline rule > card; never wrap a row in a bordered card; this is the evidence for typography-over-chrome.' boothcheck's card/shadow/radius rules are an application of this; not a gap (radius-cap 8px and shadow-lg-max are house specifics, not generalizable floors).
